message:
fix(icons): replace emoji with ASCII-safe glyphs for terminal compatibility

Replace emoji characters with monospace-safe Unicode or ASCII glyphs
to prevent rendering issues in terminals without emoji support.

Icon replacements in Unicode and NerdFonts sets:
- Sparkle: "✨" -> "✦" (4-pointed star, single-width)
- Fire: "🔥" -> "▲" (triangle, single-width)
- Rocket: "🚀" -> "➜" (arrow, single-width)
- Folder: "📁" -> "▣" (square with fill)
- File: "📄" -> "▤" (square with horizontal fill)
- Search: "🔍" -> "⌕" (search symbol)
- Save: "💾" -> "⤓" (downward arrow)
- View: "👁" -> "◉" (fisheye)
- User: "👤" -> "U" (letter)
- Investigation: "🔍" -> "⌕" (search symbol)

New WithFallback() method:
- IconSet.WithFallback(fallback IconSet) merges two icon sets
- Uses reflection to iterate over string fields
- Fills empty fields from fallback set
- Enables graceful degradation: NerdFonts.WithFallback(Unicode)

Benefits:
- Consistent single-character-width rendering across terminals
- No more misaligned columns from double-width emoji
- Works in terminals without emoji font support
- tmux copy-mode no longer garbles icon characters

Test additions:
- Tests for WithFallback() behavior
- Validation that all icon set fields are populated

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
 		BorderForeground(borderColor).
-		Width(width - 2).   // Account for borders
-		Height(height - 2). // Account for borders
+		Width(width-2).   // Account for borders
+		Height(height-2). // Account for borders
 		Padding(0, tokens.PaddingInline)
 }
